paper_metadata.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
paper_metadata.abstract: We introduce LLaMA, a collection of foundation language models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters. We train our models on trillions of tokens, and show that it is possible to train state-of-the-art models using publicly available datasets exclusively, without resorting to proprietary and inaccessible datasets. In particular, LLaMA-13B outperforms GPT-3 (175B) on most benchmarks, and LLaMA-65B is competitive with the best models, Chinchilla-70B and PaLM-540B. We release all our models to the research community.

extraction_data.innovation: LLaMA introduces a family of open and efficient foundation language models trained on trillions of tokens using publicly available datasets. The key innovation is demonstrating state-of-the-art performance with smaller models (e.g., 7B) trained on more data, challenging the assumption that larger models are always better.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: CommonCrawl (67%), C4 (15%), GitHub (4.5%), Wikipedia (4.5%), Books (4.5%), ArXiv (2.5%), StackExchange (2.0%)
extraction_data.extension: Uses RMSNorm normalization, SwiGLU activation function, and Rotary Positional Embeddings (RoPE) for improved training stability and performance.
extraction_data.research_problem: Large-scale language modeling and efficient training of open foundation models
extraction_data.application: Natural language understanding, text generation, zero-shot and few-shot learning, question answering
extraction_data.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models

extraction_data.innovation: LLaMA-I is an instruction-tuned variant of LLaMA-65B, trained to follow instructions and improve performance on downstream tasks such as question answering and dialogue. It demonstrates enhanced ability to follow complex instructions and reduce bias compared to the base model.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Common Crawl, Books1, Books2, Wikipedia, StackExchange
extraction_data.extension: Uses RMSNorm normalization, SwiGLU activation function, and Rotary Positional Embeddings (RoPE) for improved training stability and performance.
extraction_data.research_problem: Improving instruction-following and task generalization in large language models
extraction_data.application: Instruction following, multitask language understanding, dialogue systems
extraction_data.paper_title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
